name: Cereal log parser (rlog/qlog -> structured signals)
description: Add a Go package that parses openpilot's rlog and qlog files into time-series signals. rlogs are Cap'n Proto messages in the cereal schema (repo: openpilot/cereal/log.capnp at ../../openpilot/cereal/log.capnp). qlogs are a sampled subset. For a first pass, extract: carState (vEgo, steeringAngleDeg, brakePressed, gasPressed), controlsState (enabled, alertText1), deviceState (thermalStatus). Downstream features (log-signal-api, event-detector-worker) depend on this.
acceptance_criteria: internal/cereal/parser.go defines a Parser that accepts an io.Reader (a raw or bz2-compressed rlog/qlog) and yields cereal.Event objects

Use a pure-Go Cap'n Proto library: github.com/capnproto/go-capnp/v3. Generate schema bindings from openpilot/cereal/log.capnp into internal/cereal/schema/ via capnp compile (document the exact command in a top-of-file comment in parser.go)
Parser.Parse(r io.Reader, handler func(Event)) error streams events without buffering the whole file

internal/cereal/signals.go defines SignalExtractor with one method ExtractDriving(r io.Reader) (*DrivingSignals, error) that returns a struct of slices: Times []time.Time, VEgo []float64, SteeringAngleDeg []float64, BrakePressed []bool, GasPressed []bool, Engaged []bool, AlertText []string
Handles both uncompressed rlog (raw Cap'n Proto stream) and bz2-compressed (wraps Reader in bzip2.NewReader when the file is .bz2 or magic matches)

internal/cereal/parser_test.go includes a small fixture rlog (generate with a tiny Python script documented in testdata/README -- or check in a pre-captured sample <1MB if permitted) and verifies non-empty extracted signals
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
notes: Schema bindings generated from a vendored copy of openpilot/cereal/log.capnp (plus its car/legacy/custom imports) checked in under internal/cereal/schema. Parser auto-detects bz2 and streams events without buffering. SignalExtractor pulls vEgo/steering/brake/gas from carState and engaged/alertText from SelfdriveState (with fallback to deprecated ControlsState fields for older logs). deviceState.thermalStatus is not part of DrivingSignals per the signature in the acceptance criteria; callers that need it can use the lower-level Parser. Zstd logs are rejected explicitly with ErrZstdUnsupported.
